Optional keepGoing

keepGoing: undefined | false | true

deprecated

use ordered instead

Optional maxTimeMS

maxTimeMS: undefined | number

Inherited from CommandOperationOptions.maxTimeMS

  • Defined in src/operations/command.ts:31

Optional minInternalBufferSize

minInternalBufferSize: undefined | number

Inherited from OpResponseOptions.minInternalBufferSize

  • Defined in node_modules/@types/bson/index.d.ts:22

{default:1024102417}, minimum size of the internal temporary serialization buffer.

Optional ordered

ordered: undefined | false | true
  • Defined in src/operations/insert.ts:17

If true, when an insert fails, don't execute the remaining writes. If false, continue with remaining inserts when one fails.

Optional promoteBuffers

promoteBuffers: undefined | false | true

Inherited from OpResponseOptions.promoteBuffers

  • Defined in src/types.ts:22

Promotes Binary BSON values to native Node Buffers

Optional promoteLongs

promoteLongs: undefined | false | true

Inherited from OpResponseOptions.promoteLongs

Const groupFunction

groupFunction: "function () {var c = db[ns].find(condition);var map = new Map();var reduce_function = reduce;while (c.hasNext()) {var obj = c.next();var key = {};for (var i = 0, len = keys.length; i < len; ++i) {var k = keys[i];key[k] = obj[k];}var aggObj = map.get(key);if (aggObj == null) {var newObj = Object.extend({}, key);aggObj = Object.extend(newObj, initial);map.put(key, aggObj);}reduce_function(obj, aggObj);}return { "result": map.values() };}" = "function () {var c = db[ns].find(condition);var map = new Map();var reduce_function = reduce;while (c.hasNext()) {var obj = c.next();var key = {};for (var i = 0, len = keys.length; i < len; ++i) {var k = keys[i];key[k] = obj[k];}var aggObj = map.get(key);if (aggObj == null) {var newObj = Object.extend({}, key);aggObj = Object.extend(newObj, initial);map.put(key, aggObj);}reduce_function(obj, aggObj);}return { "result": map.values() };}"
  • Defined in src/operations/group.ts:74

applyRetryableWrites

  • applyRetryableWrites(target: any, db: any): any
    • Defined in src/utils.ts:350

    Applies retryWrites: true to a command if retryWrites is set on the command's database.

    Parameters

    • target: any

      The target command to which we will apply retryWrites.

    • db: any

      The database from which we can inherit a retryWrites value.

    Returns any

eachAsync

  • eachAsync<T, E>(arr: T[], eachFn: (item: T, callback: Callback<CallbackWithType<E>>) => void, callback: CallbackWithType<E>): void
    • Defined in src/utils.ts:681

    Applies the function eachFn to each item in arr, in parallel.

    Type parameters

    • T

    • E

    Parameters

    • arr: T[]

      an array of items to asynchronously iterate over

    • eachFn: (item: T, callback: Callback<CallbackWithType<E>>) => void

      A function to call on each item of the array. The callback signature is (item, callback), where the callback indicates iteration is complete.

        • (item: T, callback: Callback<CallbackWithType<E>>): void

        • Parameters

          • item: T
          • callback: Callback<CallbackWithType<E>>

          Returns void

    • callback: CallbackWithType<E>

      The callback called after every item has been iterated

    Returns void

Const executeLegacyOperation

  • executeLegacyOperation(topology: any, operation: Function, args: any, options?: any): any
    • Defined in src/utils.ts:266

    Executes the given operation with provided arguments.

    This method reduces large amounts of duplication in the entire codebase by providing a single point for determining whether callbacks or promises should be used. Additionally it allows for a single point of entry to provide features such as implicit sessions, which are required by the Driver Sessions specification in the event that a ClientSession is not provided

    Parameters

    • topology: any

      The topology to execute this operation on

    • operation: Function

      The operation to execute

    • args: any

      Arguments to apply the provided operation

    • Optional options: any

    Returns any

  • executeOperation<T>(topology: Topology, operation: T): Promise<ResultType<T["execute"]>>
  • executeOperation<T>(topology: Topology, operation: T, callback: Callback<ResultType<T["execute"]>>): void
  • executeOperation<T>(topology: Topology, operation: T, callback?: Callback<ResultType<T["execute"]>>): Promise<ResultType<T["execute"]>> | void
    • Defined in src/operations/execute_operation.ts:35

    Executes the given operation with provided arguments.

    This method reduces large amounts of duplication in the entire codebase by providing a single point for determining whether callbacks or promises should be used. Additionally it allows for a single point of entry to provide features such as implicit sessions, which are required by the Driver Sessions specification in the event that a ClientSession is not provided

    Type parameters

    • T: OperationBase

    Parameters

    • topology: Topology

      The topology to execute this operation on

    • operation: T

      The operation to execute

    Returns Promise<ResultType<T["execute"]>>

    • Defined in src/operations/execute_operation.ts:39

isSDAMUnrecoverableError

  • isSDAMUnrecoverableError(error: any): boolean
    • Defined in src/error.ts:367

    Determines whether SDAM can recover from a given error. If it cannot then the pool will be cleared, and server state will completely reset locally.

    see

    https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#not-master-and-node-is-recovering

    Parameters

    • error: any

    Returns boolean

makeInterruptableAsyncInterval

  • makeInterruptableAsyncInterval(fn: Function, options?: Partial<InterruptableAsyncIntervalOptions>): InterruptableAsyncInterval
    • Defined in src/utils.ts:910

    Creates an interval timer which is able to be woken up sooner than the interval. The timer will also debounce multiple calls to wake ensuring that the function is only ever called once within a minimum interval window.

    Parameters

    • fn: Function

      An async function to run on an interval, must accept a callback as its only parameter

    • Optional options: Partial<InterruptableAsyncIntervalOptions>

    Returns InterruptableAsyncInterval

  • maxStalenessReducer(readPreference: ReadPreference, topologyDescription: TopologyDescription, servers: ServerDescription[]): ServerDescription[]
    • Defined in src/sdam/server_selection.ts:39

    Reduces the passed in array of servers by the rules of the "Max Staleness" specification found here: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/max-staleness/max-staleness.rst

    Parameters

    • readPreference: ReadPreference

      The read preference providing max staleness guidance

    • topologyDescription: TopologyDescription

      The topology description

    • servers: ServerDescription[]

      The list of server descriptions to be reduced

    Returns ServerDescription[]

    The list of servers that satisfy the requirements of max staleness
